title: Preserve segregation of duties
description: Split the steps of a sensitive operation so no single principal can both initiate and authorize it.
body: Use when one role could perform an entire sensitive workflow end to end (request and approve a payout, grant and use elevated access, write and deploy to prod). Do not collapse initiation and authorization into one permission. Actions: identify the conflicting capability pair, ensure the permission model forces two distinct principals, and detect role assignments that recombine them. Validation signals include a test that the same principal cannot both create and approve a given request, and a report of any user holding both halves of a conflicting pair. Risk boundary: a single principal able to complete a sensitive workflow alone is a control breach; flag the toxic role combination and escalate.
